title: IntegrationConfig
description:
AirPipe integration configuration. Defines the structure for API configurations
with interfaces, actions, and global settings.

## Example
```yaml
name: my-api-config           # Required: Name of the configuration
version: 0.1.0                # Optional: Version string
description: My API config    # Optional: Description

global:                       # Optional: Global settings
  variables:                  # Global variables accessible in all interfaces
    api_base_url: https://api.example.com
  secrets:                    # Global secrets (values are encrypted)
    my_secret:
      value: secret_value
  databases:                  # Global database connections
    main_db:
      connection_string: postgres://user:pass@localhost/db

interfaces:                   # Required: Map of interface definitions
  myEndpoint:                 # Interface name (if http used as route, unless route itself is defined)
    method: POST              # HTTP method
    actions:                  # Actions to execute
      - name: ValidateInput
        input: a|body
```

expose_metrics.description:
Expose a Prometheus `/metrics` endpoint for this config (self-hosted modes only).
When true, interface and action-level metrics are emitted with `{config, interface}` labels.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this configuration does.

docs.description:
Enable or disable auto-generated API documentation for this config's HTTP interfaces.
Defaults to enabled (omit or set true). Set to false to exclude from docs.

interfaces.description:
Map of interface definitions. Keys are interface names (used as routes),
values contain the interface configuration with actions and settings.

global.description:
Global configuration shared across all interfaces including variables,
secrets, databases, templates, and HTTP defaults.

network.description:
Config-wide network access-control policy (IP/geo/ASN/rate-limit). Applies
to every interface as a baseline; each interface inherits and tightens it
via its own `network` block (unless it sets `inherit: false`).

Interface.description:
Air Pipe interface definition. An interface represents an endpoint that can be
invoked (via HTTP, schedule, etc.) and executes a chain of actions.

## Example
```yaml
myApiEndpoint:              # Interface name (can be used as route path)
  summary: Get users        # Short description for documentation
  description: |            # Long description (optional)
    Retrieves a list of all
    users in the organization.
  method: POST              # HTTP method (for HTTP interfaces)
  route: /users             # URL path (optional, defaults to interface name)
  tags:                     # OpenAPI tags for grouping
    - Users
    - API
  output: http              # Output type: http

  templates:                # Interface-specific response templates
    success_template: |
      { "status": "ok" }

  actions:                  # Ordered list of actions to execute
    - name: ValidateJwt     # Action name (used for references)
        input: a|headers    # Input source (a|headers, a|body, etc.)
      - name: FetchData
        database: main      # Database connection to use, assuming global is defined
        query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE org_uuid = $1
        params:
          - a|CheckBody::organization_uuid|

  assert:                   # Final assertion against action results
    tests:
      - value: count()
        is_equal_to: 1

  response:                 # Response configuration
    http_code_on_error: 400 # HTTP status code on error
    http_code_inherit_error: [FetchData]  # Inherit error code from action
```

disable_fastpath.description:
Opt this interface out of the compiled fast path (AP_FASTPATH), forcing every request through
the interpreter. The fast path is byte-identical, so this never changes output — it is an escape
hatch for interfaces where the fast-path attempt's side effects matter on a fallback: a fast-path
attempt that bails (an assert fails) has already fired its side-effecting actions (http requests,
DB writes), and the interpreter then re-runs them, so those side effects fire twice. Set this on
an interface where that double-fire is unacceptable (or, as here, on a test-harness interface
that deliberately fails tests so it would always bail). Default (unset) = fast path allowed.

request_example.description: Example request body shown in generated API documentation.

response_example.description: Example successful response body shown in generated API documentation.

notes.description: Additional free-form documentation notes shown under the endpoint description.

network.description:
Network access-control policy for this interface (IP/geo/ASN/rate-limit).
Inherits and tightens the config-wide `network` policy unless `inherit: false`.
Evaluated before any actions run; denied requests get the `on_deny` response.

mcp.description:
Expose this interface as an MCP tool (opt-in). When set with `enabled: true`,
the interface is listed by the MCP server (`tools/list`) and callable via
`tools/call`, reusing the interface's existing in-config auth assertions.
Absence = not exposed, so admin/sensitive interfaces simply omit this field.

Schedule.description:
AirPipe schedule configuration. Defines when and how an interface should be
executed automatically via the scheduler.

## Example
```yaml
myScheduledEndpoint:        # Interface name
  method: POST
  actions:
    - name: MyAction
      http:
        url: https://api.example.com/trigger

  schedule:                 # Schedule configuration (enables auto-execution)
    cron: "0 9 * * *"       # Required: Cron expression (5 fields: minute-hour-day-month-weekday)
    enabled: true           # Optional: Enable/disable the schedule (default: false)

    # Retry configuration
    max_attempts: 3         # Optional: Max retry attempts on failure (default: 1)
    retry_backoff_seconds: 60    # Optional: Initial backoff in seconds (default: 60)
    retry_backoff_multiplier: 2.0 # Optional: Backoff multiplier for exponential backoff (default: 2.0)
    max_backoff_seconds: 3600    # Optional: Maximum backoff cap in seconds (default: 3600)
```

cron.description: Cron expression in 5-field format (minute hour day month weekday)

max_backoff_seconds.description: Maximum retry backoff cap in seconds

timezone.description: IANA timezone for cron interpretation (e.g. 'America/New_York'); defaults to UTC

timeout.description:
Maximum execution time for this action in milliseconds.

## Example
```yaml
timeout: 5000    # 5 seconds
```

input.description:
Specify the input source for the action. Common sources:
- `a|body|` - HTTP request body
- `a|params|` - URL parameters
- `a|action_name::field|` - Output from a previous action

## Example
```yaml
input: a|body
```

## Example
```yaml
input: a|LoginBody::email
```

input_fallbacks.description:
Alternative input sources to try if the primary input is not available.

## Example
```yaml
input_fallbacks:
  - a|header::Authorization|
  - a|params::token|
```

conditional_input.description:
Define conditional input based on runtime conditions.
Uses Simple (list of input strings) or Complex (with tests).

## Example - Simple
```yaml
conditional_input:
  - a|body::premium|      # Try this first
  - a|body::standard|     # Fallback
```

## Example - Complex
```yaml
conditional_input:
  - input: a|body::type|
    tests:
      - is_equal_to: "premium"
```

pre_log.description:
Log messages before action execution.

## Example
```yaml
pre_log:
  - msg: "Starting validation"
    level: info
```

post_log.description:
Log messages after successful action completion.

## Example
```yaml
post_log:
  - msg: "Validation succeeded"
    level: info
```

error_log.description:
Log messages when action fails.

## Example
```yaml
error_log:
  - msg: "Validation failed"
    level: error
```

success_log.description:
Log messages when action succeeds.

## Example
```yaml
success_log:
  - msg: "Email sent"
    level: info
```

retry_log.description:
Log messages when action retry is attempted.

## Example
```yaml
retry_log:
  - msg: "Retrying..."
    level: warn
```

retry.description:
Configure automatic retry behavior.

## Example
```yaml
retry:
  attempts: 3           # Number of retry attempts
  delay: 1000           # Delay between retries (ms)
  exponential_backoff: true
```

lookup.description:
Name of a lookup configuration to use for pre-fetching data.

## Example
```yaml
lookup: user_lookup
```

item_timeout.description:
Per-item execution timeout for lookup iterations.
Controls how long each individual item in the lookup array is allowed to run.
Use `timeout` to cap the total loop duration.

## Example
```yaml
item_timeout: 30s
```

lookup_concurrency.description:
Maximum number of lookup items to execute concurrently.
Defaults to 10. Capped at 50 in managed run modes.

## Example
```yaml
lookup_concurrency: 20
```

lookup_partition.description:
Separate per-item successes from failures in the lookup output. When true, the
action returns `{ "succeeded": [...item data...], "failed": [{ item, http_code?, error?, data? }] }`
instead of a flat array, so a poller can advance its cursor / mark items `seen`
only for successes and let failed items be retried on the next run. Failed items
are those whose iteration returned an HTTP code >= 400 or timed out.

## Example
```yaml
lookup_partition: true
```

lookup_inherit_jq.description:
JQ transformations to apply to lookup results.

## Example
```yaml
lookup_inherit_jq:
  token: .result.token
```

lookup_inherit.description:
Fields to inherit from lookup results.

## Example
```yaml
lookup_inherit:
  api_key: lookup.api_key
```

actions.description:
Nested actions for lookup.

## Example
```yaml
actions:
  - name: GetToken
    http:
      url: https://auth.example.com
```

json_output.description:
Capture specific JSON values from output.

## Example
```yaml
json_output:
  user_id: .data.id
```

command.description:
Execute a system command.

## Example
```yaml
command:
  run: "echo hello"
  shell: bash
```

output_stdout.description:
Include command stdout in output.

## Example
```yaml
output_stdout: true
```

http.description:
HTTP request configuration.

## Example
```yaml
http:
  url: https://api.example.com
  method: POST
  headers:
    Content-Type: application/json
  body:
    key: value
```

database.description:
Database name for SQL operations.

## Example
```yaml
database: main
query: SELECT * FROM users
```

google.description:
Google API integration.

## Example
```yaml
google:
  credential: my_google_cred
  get_signed_upload_url:
    bucket: my-bucket
    key: file.txt
```

aws.description:
AWS service integration.

## Example
```yaml
aws:
  credential: my_aws_cred
  get_signed_upload_url:
    bucket: my-bucket
    key: file.txt
```

state.description:
Persistent state operation (durable key/value): polling cursors, dedupe sets,
idempotency keys, counters. Backed by a pluggable backend (in-memory for local
single-node, Postgres for durable/shared, the AirPipe backend in managed mode).
Read state inline with `a|state::KEY|`.

## Example
```yaml
state:
  advance:
    key: last_seen
    value: a|Fetch::max_updated_at|
```

delay.description:
Pause for a fixed duration before continuing — pacing / throttling between steps.
Accepts a duration string (`"2s"`, `"500ms"`, `"1m"`) or a number of milliseconds.
Bounded by `AIRPIPE__MAX_DELAY_SECS` (default 300s); longer values are rejected.
This is an in-process wait, not a durable long-running delay.

## Example
```yaml
delay: "2s"
```

email.description:
Email sending action.

## Example
```yaml
email:
  from: noreply@example.com
  to: user@example.com
  subject: Hello
  text: Body text
```

emit_metric.description:
Emit a Prometheus metric as part of this action step.
Requires `expose_metrics: true` on the parent config.

## Example
```yaml
- name: TrackRevenue
  emit_metric:
    name: revenue_total
    type: gauge
    value: a|OrderAction::amount|
    labels:
      tier: a|OrderAction::tier|
```

action.description:
Action identifier for referencing outputs.

## Example
```yaml
action: ValidateUser
# Reference later: a|ValidateUser::result|
```

depends_on.description:
Run this action when specified actions complete.
Can be a list of action names or a RunCondition config.

## Example - Simple list
```yaml
depends_on:
  - LoginBody
  - InputValidation
```

## Example - Config with at_least
```yaml
depends_on:
  at_least: 1
  actions:
    - OptionalStep1
    - OptionalStep2
```

run_when_succeeded.description:
Run this action when specified actions succeed.

## Example
```yaml
run_when_succeeded:
  - PreviousAction
```

run_when_failed.description:
Run this action when specified actions fail.

## Example
```yaml
run_when_failed:
  - MainAction
```

run_on_assertion.description:
Run this action based on assertion results.

## Example
```yaml
run_on_assertion:
  tests:
    - jq: .valid
      is_equal_to: true
```

params.description:
Parameters for SQL queries.

## Example
```yaml
params:
  - a|body::user_id|
  - "pending"
```

post_transforms.description:
Transformations to apply to action output.

## Example
```yaml
post_transforms:
  - extract_with_jq: ".[0]"
```

description.description:
Description of the action.

## Example
```yaml
description: "Fetches user details"
```

query.description:
SQL query for database actions.

## Example
```yaml
query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1
```

multi.description:
Run this action's `query` as a multi-statement batch (Postgres only).

The native driver prepares every query, and a prepared statement can hold
only one command — so a `query` with several `;`-separated statements
(e.g. a schema/seed block) normally fails with SQLSTATE 42601. Set
`multi: true` to run it via the simple protocol instead, which executes
all statements. Only valid for queries with **no** `params` (a batch
cannot be parameterized); it returns an empty result set.

## Example
```yaml
multi: true
query: |
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS a (id int);
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS b (id int);
```

url.description:
URL for HTTP actions.

## Example
```yaml
url: https://api.example.com/users
```

conn_string.description:
Database connection string override.

## Example
```yaml
conn_string: postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db
```

output.description:
Output destination handler.

## Example
```yaml
output: http
```

assert.description:
Validation and response configuration.

## Example
```yaml
assert:
  tests:
    - jq: .status
      is_equal_to: "success"
  success_message: "OK"
  error_message: "Failed"
```

document_operation.description:
Document database operations.

## Example
```yaml
document_operation:
  database: users_db
  collection: profiles
  operation: insertOne
  insert:
    name: test
```

hide_action.description:
Hide entire action data.

## Example
```yaml
hide_action: true
```

hide_data_on_success.description:
Hide action data on success.

## Example
```yaml
hide_data_on_success: true
```

hide_data_on_error.description:
Hide action data on error.

## Example
```yaml
hide_data_on_error: true
```

hide_data_on_empty.description:
Hide action data when empty.

## Example
```yaml
hide_data_on_empty: true
```

hide_errors.description:
Hide error details.

## Example
```yaml
hide_errors: true
```

hide_metrics.description:
Disable metrics collection.

## Example
```yaml
hide_metrics: true
```

response_on_success.description:
Custom response on success.

## Example
```yaml
response_on_success:
  http_code: 200
  body:
    status: success
```

response_on_error.description:
Custom response on error.

## Example
```yaml
response_on_error:
  http_code: 500
  body:
    status: error
```

Test.description:
Test definition for asserting conditions against a resolved value.
Tests support value resolution (via `value`, `jq`, `pointer`, or `action`),
type-aware comparisons, pattern matching, and structural schema validation.

When used inside an `assert` block, multiple tests run concurrently.
When used inside `verify_schema` via `FieldType.validate`, the test
runs against the leaf value at that schema path.

## Example - Basic value checks
```yaml
tests:
  - value: email
    is_not_empty: true
    regex: "^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$"
    error_message: "Invalid email"
    http_code_on_error: 422
```

## Example - Numeric comparisons
```yaml
tests:
  - value: age
    is_greater_than: 0
    is_less_than: 150
  - value: count()
    is_greater_than_or_equal_to: 1
```

## Example - Using jq for nested access
```yaml
tests:
  - jq: ".data.users | length"
    is_greater_than: 0
```

## Example - Cross-action reference
```yaml
tests:
  - action: FetchUser
    value: email
    is_not_null: true
```

## Example - Allowed/disallowed values
```yaml
tests:
  - value: status
    contains:
      allowed:
        - "active"
        - "pending"
        - "suspended"
      disallowed:
        - "deleted"
```

## Example - Schema validation with deep nested assertions
```yaml
tests:
  - verify_schema:
      disallow_unknown_keys: true
      structure:
        email:
          type: String
          validate:
            is_not_empty: true
            regex: "^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$"
        age:
          type: Number
          validate:
            is_greater_than: 0
        settings:
          theme:
            type: String
          notifications:
            type: Boolean
```

name.description: Human-readable name for identifying this test in logs and error output.

description.description: Description of what this test validates.

value.description:
JSON path to the target value within the input data (e.g., `email`, `user.name`).
Also supports `count()` to get the length of an array.

## Example
```yaml
value: email             # simple key
value: user.address.city # nested path
value: count()           # array length
```

action.description:
Reference a previous action's output as the target value.

## Example
```yaml
action: FetchUser
value: email          # key within that action's output
```

action_value.description:
Reference a previous action's output using interpolation syntax.

## Example
```yaml
action_value: a|FetchUser::email|
```

custom_value.description:
Override the target value with a custom literal string.

## Example
```yaml
custom_value: "some_static_value"
is_equal_to: "some_static_value"
```

jq.description:
Extract the target value using a jq filter expression.

## Example
```yaml
jq: ".users[0].email"
is_not_empty: true
```

pointer.description:
Extract the target value using a JSON pointer (RFC 6901).

## Example
```yaml
pointer: "/data/0/email"
is_not_empty: true
```

http_code_on_error.description:
HTTP status code to return when this specific test fails.
Takes precedence over the parent `Assert.http_code_on_error`.

success_message.description: Message returned when this test succeeds.

error_message.description:
Custom error message when this test fails. Replaces the default
auto-generated error detail.

## Example
```yaml
value: email
is_not_empty: true
error_message: "Email address is required"
```

hide_errors.description: Suppress error details from the response output for this test.

http_response.description: Static HTTP response value to return when this test is evaluated.

case_insensitive.description:
Enable case-insensitive matching for `is_equal_to` and `contains` checks.

## Example
```yaml
value: status
is_equal_to: "active"
case_insensitive: true    # matches "Active", "ACTIVE", etc.
```

is_equal_to.description:
Assert the value equals the expected value. Behavior varies by type:
strings compare as text, numbers as numeric value, booleans as bool,
arrays and objects compare as full JSON equality.

## Example
```yaml
is_equal_to: "active"      # string
is_equal_to: 42            # number
is_equal_to: true          # boolean
```

is_not_equal_to.description:
Assert the value does not equal the expected value.

## Example
```yaml
is_not_equal_to: "deleted"
```

is_less_than.description:
Assert the value is less than the threshold. For strings and arrays,
compares the length. For numbers, compares the numeric value.

## Example
```yaml
# String: length < 256
value: title
is_less_than: 256

# Number: value < 100
value: percentage
is_less_than: 100

# Array: length < 10
value: items
is_less_than: 10
```

is_less_than_or_equal_to.description:
Assert the value is less than or equal to the threshold.
Same type-dependent behavior as `is_less_than`.

is_greater_than.description:
Assert the value is greater than the threshold.
Same type-dependent behavior as `is_less_than`.

## Example
```yaml
value: age
is_greater_than: 0
```

is_greater_than_or_equal_to.description:
Assert the value is greater than or equal to the threshold.
Same type-dependent behavior as `is_less_than`.

starts_with.description:
Assert the string value starts with the given prefix.

## Example
```yaml
value: url
starts_with: "https://"
```

ends_with.description:
Assert the string value ends with the given suffix.

## Example
```yaml
value: filename
ends_with: ".pdf"
```

contains_text.description: Assert the string contains at least one alphabetic character.

contains_number.description: Assert the string contains at least one numeric digit.

contains_upper.description: Assert the string contains at least one uppercase letter.

contains_lower.description: Assert the string contains at least one lowercase letter.

contains_special.description: Assert the string contains at least one special character.

contains.description:
Assert containment. Behavior varies by target type:
- **String**: substring match (or exact match in advanced/any mode)
- **Number**: substring match against the string representation
- **Array**: element membership
- **Object**: key existence

Supports three modes: standard (single value or array), advanced
(with `at_least` threshold), and any (with `allowed`/`disallowed` lists).

## Example - String substring
```yaml
value: email
contains: "@"
```

## Example - Array membership
```yaml
value: tags
contains: "featured"
```

## Example - Allowed values
```yaml
value: status
contains:
  allowed:
    - "active"
    - "pending"
  disallowed:
    - "deleted"
```

## Example - At least N matches
```yaml
value: roles
contains:
  values:
    - "admin"
    - "editor"
    - "viewer"
  at_least: 1
```

like.description:
SQL LIKE-style pattern matching (case-sensitive). Uses `%` as wildcard.

## Example
```yaml
value: name
like: "John%"         # starts with "John"
```

ilike.description:
SQL ILIKE-style pattern matching (case-insensitive). Uses `%` as wildcard.

## Example
```yaml
value: email
ilike: "%@gmail.com"  # any Gmail address
```

not_contains.description:
Inverse of `contains`. Assert the value does NOT contain the specified
content. Same mode support as `contains` (standard, advanced, any).

## Example
```yaml
value: content
not_contains: "<script>"
```

regex.description:
Assert the string value matches a regular expression pattern.

## Example
```yaml
value: phone
regex: "^\\+?[1-9]\\d{1,14}$"
```

is_not_null.description:
Assert the value is not null.

## Example
```yaml
value: user_id
is_not_null: true
```

is_not_empty.description:
Assert the value is not empty. Checks strings, arrays, and objects.

## Example
```yaml
value: name
is_not_empty: true
```

is_null.description:
Assert the value is null (when `true`) or is not null (when `false`).

## Example
```yaml
value: deleted_at
is_null: true         # must be null

value: email
is_null: false         # must not be null
```

is_uuid.description:
Assert the value is a valid UUID string.

## Example
```yaml
value: id
is_uuid: true
```

is_array.description:
Assert the value is a JSON array.

## Example
```yaml
value: items
is_array: true
```

is_valid_jwt.description:
Validate the value as a JWT token using the provided secret.
On success, decoded claims are stored in `ok_data` under the
key specified by `result_key` (default: `jwt_claims`).

## Example
```yaml
value: token
is_valid_jwt: "my_jwt_secret"       # HS256 secret (bare string), or an object:
# is_valid_jwt: { jwks_url: "...", alg: RS256, iss: "...", aud: "..." }
result_key: user_claims
```

is_valid_hmac.description:
Verify an HMAC signature. The `value` field should point to the signature
received in the request (e.g. `x-hub-signature-256`). The assertion
recomputes HMAC over `body` using `secret` and compares against the target.

## Example (GitHub webhook)
```yaml
value: x-hub-signature-256
is_valid_hmac:
  secret: a|env::GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET|
  body: a|raw_body|
  algorithm: sha256   # sha1 | sha256 | sha512  (default: sha256)
  prefix: "sha256="   # stripped from target before comparison
```

is_authorization.description:
Parse an HTTP Authorization header value. Supports `Basic` (decodes
base64 username:password) and `Bearer` (extracts token). Decoded
data is stored in `ok_data` keyed by scheme name.

## Example
```yaml
value: authorization
is_authorization: true
```

result_key.description:
Key name for storing extracted data (e.g., JWT claims) in the
action's ok_data output.

expression.description:
Evaluate a string expression as a boolean condition.
Supports interpolation via `a|...|` syntax before evaluation.

## Example
```yaml
expression: "a|body::count| > 0"
```

compare_value.description:
Compare the target value against another value or file for structural
differences. Supports JSON, XML, and string comparison with optional
field exclusions.

## Example - Compare against inline target
```yaml
compare_value:
  target: '{"status": "ok"}'
  data_type: json
  ignore_fields:
    - timestamp
```

## Example - Compare against file
```yaml
compare_value:
  file_path: "/path/to/expected.json"
  data_type: json
```

bcrypt_verify.description: Verify a plaintext value against a bcrypt hash. Deprecated: use `verify_bcrypt`.

verify_bcrypt.description:
Verify a plaintext value against a bcrypt hash.

## Example
```yaml
value: password
verify_bcrypt: "$2b$12$LJ3m..."
```

verify_signature.description:
Verify an Ed25519 signature against the target value.

## Example
```yaml
value: request_body
verify_signature:
  public_key: "a1b2c3..."    # hex-encoded Ed25519 public key
  signature: "d4e5f6..."     # hex-encoded signature
```

verify_schema.description:
Validate the value against a structural schema with recursive type
checking and optional per-field assertions. Supports nested objects,
arrays, and leaf-level validation via the `validate` field on each
`FieldType`.

## Example - Flat structure
```yaml
verify_schema:
  disallow_unknown_keys: true
  structure:
    name:
      type: String
      validate:
        is_not_empty: true
    age:
      type: Number
      validate:
        is_greater_than: 0
    active:
      type: Boolean
```

## Example - Nested objects with validation
```yaml
verify_schema:
  structure:
    user:
      email:
        type: String
        validate:
          is_not_empty: true
          regex: "^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$"
      settings:
        theme:
          type: String
          validate:
            contains:
              allowed:
                - "light"
                - "dark"
                - "system"
        notifications:
          type: Boolean
```

## Example - Array with positional validation
```yaml
verify_schema:
  structure:
    - id:
        type: Number
        validate:
          is_greater_than: 0
      title:
        type: String
        validate:
          is_not_empty: true
          is_less_than: 256
```

semver_matches.description:
Assert the string value satisfies a semver version constraint.

## Example
```yaml
value: api_version
semver_matches: ">=1.0.0, <2.0.0"
```

size_limit.description:
Assert the serialized byte size of the value does not exceed a limit.
Accepts human-readable sizes (e.g., `1 MB`, `500 KB`) or raw byte counts.

## Example
```yaml
value: payload
size_limit: "1 MB"
```

ContainOptions.description:
Containment check options. Supports three modes depending on the YAML structure
provided: standard (single value or array), advanced (with `at_least` threshold),
and any (with `allowed`/`disallowed` lists).

## Example - Standard single value
```yaml
contains: "search_term"
```

## Example - Standard array (match at least one)
```yaml
contains:
  - "admin"
  - "editor"
```

## Example - Advanced (at least N matches)
```yaml
contains:
  values:
    - "read"
    - "write"
    - "delete"
  at_least: 2
```

## Example - Allowed/disallowed lists
```yaml
contains:
  allowed:
    - "active"
    - "pending"
  disallowed:
    - "banned"
```

anyOf.description:
Allowed/disallowed list mode. Target must match an allowed value
and must not match any disallowed value.
Advanced mode with explicit value list and minimum match threshold.
Standard mode: a single value or array of values to check for containment.

ContainsAny.description:
Allowed/disallowed containment check. For strings, checks exact match against
each list. For arrays, checks that all elements are in `allowed` and no
elements are in `disallowed`.

## Example
```yaml
contains:
  allowed:
    - "published"
    - "draft"
    - "archived"
  disallowed:
    - "deleted"
    - "banned"
```

allowed.description: Values the target is allowed to match.

disallowed.description: Values the target must not match.

ContainsAdv.description:
Advanced containment check with a minimum match threshold.

## Example
```yaml
contains:
  values:
    - "read"
    - "write"
    - "admin"
  at_least: 2             # target must match at least 2 of the listed values
```

values.description: List of candidate values to check against.

at_least.description: Minimum number of values that must match for the check to pass.

JwtConfig.description:
Configuration for the `is_valid_jwt` assertion.

Accepts either a bare string (the HS256 shared secret — the original form,
still supported) or an object for asymmetric algorithms and OIDC providers:

```yaml
# HS256 (unchanged):
is_valid_jwt: "my-shared-secret"

# RS256/ES256 with a static PEM public key:
is_valid_jwt:
  alg: RS256
  public_key: a|ap_var::IDP_PUBLIC_KEY|

# RS256/ES256 verified against a provider's rotating JWKS (Auth0/Clerk/Cognito):
is_valid_jwt:
  jwks_url: https://YOUR.auth0.com/.well-known/jwks.json
  alg: RS256          # optional; falls back to the token header's alg
  iss: https://YOUR.auth0.com/
  aud: https://your-api
```

anyOf.description: Structured verification config for HS*/RS*/ES*/EdDSA and JWKS.

JwtVerifyConfig.description:
Structured form of [`JwtConfig`] — a symmetric secret, a static public key,
or a JWKS URL, plus optional algorithm and issuer/audience checks.

secret.description: HS256/384/512 shared secret. Mutually exclusive with `public_key`/`jwks_url`.

public_key.description: PEM-encoded public key for RS*/ES*/EdDSA verification (no network calls).

jwks_url.description:
URL of a JWKS document; the signing key is selected by the token's `kid`.
Fetched once and cached (with a short TTL), so provider key rotation is handled.

alg.description:
Signature algorithm, e.g. `HS256`, `RS256`, `ES256`, `EdDSA`. Required for
`public_key`; for `jwks_url` it defaults to the token header's `alg`.

iss.description: Expected `iss` claim. When set, tokens with a different issuer are rejected.

aud.description: Expected `aud` claim. When set, tokens with a different audience are rejected.

body.description:
The message to authenticate. Use `a|raw_body|` for webhook signature
verification so the bytes match exactly what the sender signed.

prefix.description:
Optional prefix to strip from the target value before comparing.
GitHub uses `"sha256="`, Stripe uses `"v1="`.

CompareValue.description:
Value comparison configuration for diffing against an expected value.
Supports inline target strings or file paths, with optional field exclusions.

## Example - Inline comparison
```yaml
compare_value:
  target: '{"status": "ok", "code": 200}'
  data_type: json
  ignore_fields:
    - timestamp
    - request_id
```

## Example - File comparison
```yaml
compare_value:
  file_path: "./fixtures/expected_response.json"
  data_type: json
```

target.description: Inline string value to compare against.

data_type.description: Data format for comparison parsing: `json`, `xml`, or `string`.

ignore_fields.description: Fields to exclude from the comparison.

file_path.description: Path to a file containing the expected value.

data_type.description: Data format for comparison parsing: `json`, `xml`, or `string`.

ignore_fields.description: Fields to exclude from the comparison.

VerifySignature.description:
Ed25519 signature verification parameters.

## Example
```yaml
value: request_body
verify_signature:
  public_key: "a1b2c3d4e5f6..."     # hex-encoded Ed25519 public key
  signature: "f6e5d4c3b2a1..."       # hex-encoded signature bytes
```

signature.description: Hex-encoded signature bytes to verify against the target value.

VerifySchema.description:
Schema validation configuration. Validates a JSON value against a recursive
type structure with optional per-field assertions and unknown key rejection.

## Example - Basic object validation
```yaml
verify_schema:
  disallow_unknown_keys: true
  structure:
    name:
      type: String
      validate:
        is_not_empty: true
    email:
      type: String
      validate:
        regex: "^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$"
    age:
      type: Number
      validate:
        is_greater_than: 0
        is_less_than: 150
```

## Example - Nested with unknown key rejection
```yaml
verify_schema:
  disallow_unknown_keys: true
  structure:
    user:
      name:
        type: String
      role:
        type: String
        validate:
          contains:
            allowed:
              - "admin"
              - "editor"
              - "viewer"
    metadata:
      created_at:
        type: Number
      version:
        type: String
        validate:
          semver_matches: ">=1.0.0"
```

## Example - Array of objects (positional)
```yaml
verify_schema:
  structure:
    - id:
        type: Number
        validate:
          is_greater_than: 0
      status:
        type: String
        validate:
          is_not_empty: true
      settings:
        category:
          type: String
        featured:
          type: Boolean
```

disallow_unknown_keys.description:
When `true`, any keys present in the value but not defined in the schema
are reported as errors. Applies recursively to all nested objects.

structure.description:
The expected structure definition. Can be a nested object map, a positional
array, or a single leaf field type.

ValueType.description:
Schema value type for recursive structural validation. Represents either
a leaf field with a type constraint, a nested object with named fields,
or a positional array of typed elements.

## Example - Leaf field
```yaml
email:
  type: String
  validate:
    is_not_empty: true
```

## Example - Nested object
```yaml
user:
  name:
    type: String
  settings:
    theme:
      type: String
```

## Example - Positional array
```yaml
structure:
  - name:
      type: String
    id:
      type: Number
```

anyOf.description: Leaf field with type constraint and optional validation.
Nested object where each key maps to another `ValueType`.
Positional array where each index maps to a `ValueType`.

FieldType.description:
Leaf field definition within a `verify_schema` structure. Specifies the
expected data type and optional validation assertions that run against
the resolved value at this path.

Supported types: `String`, `Number`, `Boolean`, `Array`, `Object`, `Null`, `Any`.

## Example - Type check only
```yaml
email:
  type: String
```

## Example - Type check with assertions
```yaml
rating:
  type: Number
  validate:
    is_greater_than_or_equal_to: 0
    is_less_than_or_equal_to: 5
```

## Example - String with pattern and length
```yaml
slug:
  type: String
  validate:
    is_not_empty: true
    regex: "^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$"
    is_less_than: 128
```

## Example - Enum-like string constraint
```yaml
status:
  type: String
  validate:
    contains:
      allowed:
        - "published"
        - "draft"
        - "archived"
```

validate.description:
Optional test assertions to run against the leaf value after the type
check passes. Uses the same `Test` definition as the normal assertion
system, giving full access to all checks (`is_equal_to`, `regex`,
`contains`, `is_greater_than`, etc.).

## Example
```yaml
validate:
  is_not_empty: true
  regex: "^[a-z0-9-]+$"
  is_less_than: 64
```

multipart.description:
A flat list of parts for a `multipart/form-data` request.

Each entry is one form part. Whether it is a text or binary part is determined
implicitly by which source field is provided:

- `value` → plain text part
- `b64` → binary part decoded from base64
- `file_path` → binary part read from disk (not allowed in managed/hosted mode)

`filename` and `mime` are always optional. When omitted on a binary part, `mime`
is guessed from the `filename` extension, falling back to `application/octet-stream`.

```yaml
multipart:
  - name: image
    b64: a|previous_action::body.image.data|
    filename: photo.png   # optional — sent as Content-Disposition filename
    mime: image/png       # optional — guessed from filename if absent
  - name: model
    value: gpt-image-1
  - name: prompt
    value: Transform into Studio Ghibli style
```

MultipartPart.description:
A single part in a `multipart/form-data` request.

Exactly one of `value`, `b64`, or `file_path` must be provided per part.
All other fields are optional.

name.description: Form field name — the `name` attribute sent in `Content-Disposition`.

value.description:
Plain text value. Use this for non-binary form fields.
Mutually exclusive with `b64` and `file_path`.

b64.description:
Base64-encoded binary data. Decoded before sending.
Mutually exclusive with `value` and `file_path`.

file_path.description:
Path to a file to read and send as binary data.
Not permitted in managed/hosted mode — use `b64` instead.
Mutually exclusive with `value` and `b64`.

filename.description:
Filename sent in the `Content-Disposition` header for this part (e.g. `photo.png`).
Optional for binary parts; not meaningful for plain text parts.

mime.description:
MIME type for this part (e.g. `image/png`, `application/pdf`).
If omitted on a binary part, guessed from `filename`'s extension.
Falls back to `application/octet-stream` when neither is available.

MultipartData.description: Deprecated: use [`MultipartPart`] via the `multipart` field instead.

MultipartFile.description: Deprecated: use [`MultipartPart`] via the `multipart` field instead.

MultipartText.description: Deprecated: use [`MultipartPart`] via the `multipart` field instead.

ProxyHeaders.description: Controls which headers are passed through from the incoming request.

anyOf.description: When true, pass through all incoming headers
When a list, pass through only the specified headers; empty list means pass none

StateAction.description:
A persistent state operation. State is durable key/value used for things like
polling cursors, dedupe sets, idempotency keys and counters — distinct from
`var::` variables, which are read-only config/secrets.

It is backed by a pluggable backend selected by deployment:
in-memory (local single-node, ephemeral), Postgres (durable/shared, BYO via
`global.databases`), or the AirPipe backend (managed). Redis is a planned backend.

Read state inline anywhere interpolation runs with `a|state::KEY|` or
`a|state::NAMESPACE.KEY|`. The default namespace is the config name.

## Example — read a cursor, fetch since it, advance it
```yaml
actions:
  - name: Cursor
    state:
      get:
        key: last_seen
        default: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  - name: Fetch
    http:
      url: "https://api.example.com/items?since=a|state::last_seen|"
  - name: SaveCursor
    state:
      advance:
        key: last_seen
        value: a|Fetch::max_updated_at|
```

oneOf.description:
Read a value by key. Output: `{ "value": <v>, "version": <n>, "found": <bool> }`.
Returns `default` (or null) when the key is absent or expired.

Upsert a value. Last-write-wins unless `expected_version` is set (optimistic
concurrency — the write fails with a conflict if the stored version differs).
Output: `{ "version": <n> }`.

Monotonically advance a cursor: stores `max(current, value)`. Numeric values are
compared numerically; otherwise lexically (works for ISO-8601 timestamps).
Never moves a cursor backwards, so it is safe without CAS. Output: `{ "version": <n>, "value": <v> }`.

Check-and-add an id to a bounded dedupe set. Returns whether the id was new
(i.e. not seen before). Output: `{ "was_new": <bool> }`.

namespace.description: Optional namespace. Defaults to the config name.

default.description: Value to return when the key is absent or expired.

namespace.description: Optional namespace. Defaults to the config name.

ttl.description: Optional time-to-live, e.g. "1h", "30m", "7d". Omit for no expiry.

expected_version.description:
Optimistic concurrency: only write when the stored version matches this value.
Use `0` to require the key not to exist yet. Ignored by `advance`.

key.description: Key identifying the dedupe set (e.g. the trigger/source name).

id.description: The id to check and record (e.g. a record id or content hash).

namespace.description: Optional namespace. Defaults to the config name.

ttl.description: Optional time-to-live for the set, e.g. "7d".

name.description: Metric name (must be a valid Prometheus metric name).

value.description:
Value to record. Required for `gauge` and `histogram`; counters increment by 1 when omitted.
Accepts a literal number or a standard interpolation expression (`a|ActionName::field|`).
Numeric values resolved via interpolation retain their type — no string parsing required.

labels.description:
User-defined labels. `config`, `interface`, and `action` are always attached automatically.
Values accept standard interpolation expressions. Resolved values are coerced to strings
at emit time since Prometheus labels must be strings.

Assert.description:
Assertion block for validating action output or interface results.
Groups one or more tests with shared error handling and HTTP response codes.

## Example - Action-level assertion
```yaml
actions:
  - name: ValidateBody
    input: a|body|
    assert:
      tests:
        - value: email
          is_not_empty: true
          regex: "^[^@]+@[^@]+\\.[^@]+$"
        - value: age
          is_greater_than: 0
      error_message: "Validation failed"
      http_code_on_error: 400
```

## Example - Interface-level assertion
```yaml
interfaces:
  getUser:
    method: GET
    actions:
      - name: FetchUser
        database: main
        query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1
        params:
          - a|params::id|
    assert:
      tests:
        - value: count()
          is_equal_to: 1
      error_message: "User not found"
      http_code_on_error: 404
      http_code_on_success: 200
```

error_message.description:
Message returned when any test fails. Overrides individual test error messages
at the assert level.

http_code_on_error.description:
HTTP status code to return when any test fails. Individual test-level
`http_code_on_error` takes precedence when set.

http_code_on_success.description: HTTP status code to return when all tests pass.

tests.description:
List of test definitions to evaluate. All tests run concurrently;
errors from all tests are collected and returned together.

## Example
```yaml
tests:
  - value: username
    is_not_empty: true
    error_message: "Username is required"
    http_code_on_error: 422
  - value: password
    is_not_empty: true
    contains_upper: true
    contains_number: true
    is_greater_than: 7
```

rename_attributes.description:
Rename attributes (keys) within a JSON object. Maps old attribute names to new attribute names.

## Example
```yaml
rename_attributes:
  old_name: new_name
```

remove_attributes.description:
Remove specified attributes from the JSON object. Provides an array of attribute names to exclude.

## Example
```yaml
remove_attributes:
  - password
  - secret
```

keep_attributes.description:
Keep only specified attributes, removing all others. Provides an allowlist of attribute names.

## Example
```yaml
keep_attributes:
  - id
  - name
  - email
```

move_attributes.description:
Move attributes from one location to another. Maps source path to destination path.

## Example
```yaml
move_attributes:
  user.name: name
  user.email: email
```

rename_keys.description:
Rename keys at the root level of a JSON object. Maps old key names to new key names.

## Example
```yaml
rename_keys:
  user_id: userId
  created_at: createdAt
```

remove_keys.description:
Remove specified keys from the JSON object. Provides an array of key names to exclude.

## Example
```yaml
remove_keys:
  - token
  - internal_id
```

bcrypt.description:
Hash a value using bcrypt for secure password storage. Supports custom cost factor.

## Example
```yaml
bcrypt:
  key: password          # Path to the password value (e.g., .password)
  cost: 12               # Cost factor (higher = slower but more secure)
```

add_attribute.description:
Add new attributes to the JSON object with specified key-value pairs.

## Example
```yaml
add_attribute:
  user_uuid: a|uuid|           # Auto-generate UUID
  verification_token: a|uuid|  # Auto-generate token
```

return_row.description:
Return a specific row from a result set by index (0-based).

## Example
```yaml
return_row: 0    # Return the first row
```

extract_value.description:
Extract a single value from the response using a JSON path.

## Example
```yaml
extract_value: .user.id
```

extract_array.description:
Extract an array from a JSON response with options for flattening and deduplication.

## Example
```yaml
extract_array:
  from: .items         # Path to the array
  paths:               # Paths to extract from each element
    - id
    - name
  flatten: true        # Flatten nested arrays
  dedupe: true         # Remove duplicates
```

extract_with_json_path.description:
Extract data using a JSON path expression. Shortcut for simple extractions.

## Example
```yaml
extract_with_json_path: .[0]  # Get first element
```

extract_with_jq.description:
Extract data using jq filter syntax for complex JSON transformations.

## Example
```yaml
extract_with_jq: '.users | map({id, name})'
```

extract_with_regex.description:
Extract values from a string using regex capture groups.

## Example
```yaml
extract_with_regex:
  value: .phone                           # Path to source string
  expr: "(\\d{3})-(\\d{3})-(\\d{4})"   # Regex with capture groups
  keys:                                   # Names for captured groups
    - area
    - prefix
    - number
```

add_jwt.description:
Add a JWT token to the response with configurable expiration and payload.

## Example
```yaml
add_jwt:
  key: jwt                               # Output key for the token
  secret: a|var::JWT_SECRET|            # JWT secret (use encrypted variable)
  exp: 1d                                # Expiration STRING (e.g., 1h, 1d, 30m)
  data:                                  # Claims — three accepted forms:
    sub: a|FetchUser::id|                #  • object: explicit claim -> value (most flexible)
    email: a|FetchUser::email|
  # data: [id, email]                    #  • array: copy these fields from this action's input
  # data: all                            #  • "all": copy every input field
```

**Security:** Store JWT secrets as encrypted AirPipe variables:
```yaml
secret: a|var::ENCRYPTED_JWT_SECRET|
```

read_jwt.description:
Read and decode a JWT token to extract its payload.

## Example
```yaml
read_jwt:
  key: user_data       # Output key for decoded payload
  token: .auth_header  # Path to JWT token
```

numerics.description:
Convert string fields to numeric types. Provides an array of field names to convert.

## Example
```yaml
numerics:
  - age
  - price
  - quantity
```

group_by.description:
Group array elements by specified fields. Used for aggregation operations.

## Example
```yaml
group_by:
  - category
  - region
```

generate_password.description:
Generate a secure random password with customizable options for length and character types.

## Example
```yaml
generate_password:
  key: temp_password         # Output key for generated password
  length: 16                 # Password length
  numbers: true              # Include numbers (0-9)
  lowercase_letters: true    # Include lowercase (a-z)
  uppercase_letters: true    # Include uppercase (A-Z)
  symbols: true              # Include symbols (!@#$)
```

replace_values.description:
Replace values in strings or arrays with support for regex and multiple replacements.

## Example - Simple string replacement
```yaml
replace_values:
  target_value: .message    # Path to target value
  find_str: old             # String to find
  new_str: new              # Replacement string
```

## Example - Multiple value replacement
```yaml
replace_values:
  target_value: .status
  values:                    # Array of values to process
    - pending
    - approved
  find_str: _
  new_str: " "
```

flatten.description:
Flatten nested objects into a single level with configurable key separators.

## Example
```yaml
flatten:
  key_separator: .    # Separator for nested keys
  prefix: user        # Optional prefix for all keys
  target: .data       # Path to nested object
```

b64_decode.description:
Decode a Base64-encoded string.

## Example
```yaml
b64_decode: .encoded_data    # Path to Base64 string
```

b64_decode_as_json.description:
Decode a Base64-encoded string and parse as JSON.

## Example
```yaml
b64_decode_as_json: .payload    # Path to Base64-encoded JSON
```

b64_encode.description:
Encode a value to Base64. Can be a direct string or custom transform configuration.

## Example
```yaml
b64_encode: .data    # Path to value to encode
```

yaml_to_json.description:
Convert YAML-formatted string to JSON.

## Example
```yaml
yaml_to_json: .yaml_data    # Path to YAML string
```

nested_transforms.description:
Apply nested transforms to specific keys. Maps keys to arrays of transform operations.

## Example
```yaml
nested_transforms:
  items:                    # Transform each item in the 'items' array
    - remove_keys:
        - internal_id
    - rename_keys:
        user_name: name
```

math.description:
Perform mathematical operations on numeric values with expressions.

## Example
```yaml
math:
  target: .data                    # Path to target object
  expressions:                     # Math expressions to evaluate
    total: price * quantity
    discounted: total * 0.9
  decimal_places: 2                # Round to 2 decimal places
```

filter.description:
Filter array elements based on specified conditions.

## Example
```yaml
filter:
  target: .users                   # Path to array to filter
  conditions:
    .age:
      is_greater_than: 18
    .status:
      is_equal_to: "active"
```

generate_bytes.description:
Generate random bytes with specified length.

## Example
```yaml
generate_bytes:
  key: api_key      # Output key for generated bytes
  length: 32        # Number of bytes to generate
```

encrypt_value.description:
Encrypt a value using AES-256-GCM encryption.

## Example
```yaml
encrypt_value:
  key: encrypted_data           # Output key for encrypted result
  data: .sensitive_value        # Path to value to encrypt
  secret_key: a|var::ENCRYPTED_ENCRYPTION_KEY|  # Encryption key (must be encrypted variable)
  nonce: a|var::NONCE|          # Unique nonce (must be encrypted variable)
```

**Security:**
- Store encryption keys as encrypted AirPipe variables
- Use unique nonce values for each encryption operation

decrypt_value.description:
Decrypt a value previously sealed with `encrypt_value` (AES-256-GCM).

## Example
```yaml
decrypt_value:
  key: plaintext                # Output key for the decrypted string
  value: a|Row::enc_value|      # Base64 ciphertext (the encrypt_value `.value`)
  nonce: a|Row::enc_nonce|      # Base64 nonce   (the encrypt_value `.nonce`)
  tag: a|Row::enc_tag|          # Base64 GCM tag (the encrypt_value `.tag`)
  secret_key: a|env::AIRPIPE_ENC_SECRET_KEY|  # Same key used to encrypt
```

**Security:** decryption is authenticated — a wrong key, altered ciphertext,
or altered tag fails loudly rather than returning garbage.

s3_generate_presigned_url.description:
Generate a presigned URL for S3 file access.

## Example
```yaml
s3_generate_presigned_url:
  bucket: my-bucket                         # S3 bucket name
  file_key: documents/file.pdf              # Path to file in bucket
  expiration_secs: 3600                     # URL expiration (seconds)
  endpoint: https://s3.amazonaws.com        # S3 endpoint
  region: us-east-1                         # AWS region
  access_key_id: a|var::ENCRYPTED_AWS_ACCESS_KEY|  # AWS access key (encrypted variable)
  secret_access_key: a|var::ENCRYPTED_AWS_SECRET|  # AWS secret key (encrypted variable)
  key: presigned_url                        # Output key for generated URL
  json_path: .file_url                      # Optional: Extract from response
```

**Security:** Store AWS credentials securely as encrypted AirPipe variables:
```yaml
access_key_id: a|var::ENCRYPTED_AWS_ACCESS_KEY|
secret_access_key: a|var::ENCRYPTED_AWS_SECRET|
```

encode_uri.description:
URI-encode an entire URL string.

## Example
```yaml
encode_uri: .url    # Path to URL to encode
```

encode_uri_component.description:
URI-encode a single component of a URL.

## Example
```yaml
encode_uri_component: .search_query    # Path to component to encode
```

decode_uri.description:
URI-decode an encoded URL string.

## Example
```yaml
decode_uri: .encoded_url    # Path to encoded URL
```

decode_uri_component.description:
URI-decode a single component of an encoded URL.

## Example
```yaml
decode_uri_component: .encoded_param    # Path to encoded component
```

generate_slug.description:
Generate a URL-friendly slug from a string.

## Example
```yaml
generate_slug: .title    # Path to text to convert to slug
```

from.description: The JSON path to the starting array

paths.description: Array of JSON paths to extract values from each element

conditions.description: Conditions to filter items in the array.

Param.description:
Parameter definition for interface-level URL parameters with optional
validation and default values.

## Example
```yaml
interfaces:
  getUser:
    method: GET
    params:
      id:
        value: a|params::id|
        error_message: "User ID is required"
        validate:
          is_not_empty: true
          regex: "^[0-9]+$"
      page:
        value: a|params::page|
        default_value: "1"
```

default_value.description: Default value if the parameter is not provided.

error_message.description: Custom error message when validation fails.

validate.description:
Validation test to run against the parameter value.

## Example
```yaml
validate:
  is_not_empty: true
  is_less_than: 100
  regex: "^[a-zA-Z0-9]+$"
```

NetworkPolicy.description:
Network access-control policy. Set it config-wide (top-level `network:`, applies
to every interface as a baseline) and/or per-interface (`interface.network:`,
which inherits and **tightens** the config-wide policy).

## Inheritance (tighten-only)
- `deny` lists (ip/geo/asn) **union** across levels — a config-level deny can
  never be weakened by an interface.
- `allow` lists **intersect** — the request must satisfy every level that
  declares an allow list.
- An interface sets `inherit: false` to ignore the config-wide policy entirely
  (a deliberate public exception, e.g. a health route).
- Scalar settings (`mode`, `source`, `trusted_proxies`, `on_deny`) are taken
  from the interface when present, otherwise inherited from the config level.

## Evaluation order (per level)
`ip.deny` → `geo.deny` → `asn.deny` → `*.allow` → `rate_limit`. The first rule
that rejects wins. In `monitor` mode nothing is blocked; matches are logged.

## Example
```yaml
network:                       # config-wide baseline
  mode: enforce                # enforce (default) | monitor
  source: client               # client (default) | socket | real_ip
  trusted_proxies:             # validate XFF chain (anti-spoofing)
    - "10.0.0.0/8"
  ip:
    allow: ["203.0.113.0/24", "2001:db8::/32"]
    deny:  ["1.2.3.4"]
    deny_check: source         # source (default) | all
  geo:   { deny: ["RU", "KP"] }
  asn:   { deny: [13335] }
  rate_limit: { per: ip, requests: 100, window: "60s" }
  on_deny: { status: 403, body: { error: "forbidden" } }

interfaces:
  admin:
    network:                   # inherits + tightens the baseline
      ip: { allow: ["198.51.100.10"] }
  health:
    network: { inherit: false } # public exception
```

inherit.description:
Interface-level only: when `false`, ignore the config-wide policy for this
interface. Defaults to `true`. Ignored at the config level.

source.description: Which captured IP to treat as authoritative. Defaults to `client`.

trusted_proxies.description:
Trusted proxy CIDRs. When set, the client IP is resolved by walking the
`X-Forwarded-For` chain from the right and skipping these hops, which
defeats client-supplied header spoofing. Without it, `source: client`
trusts the leftmost (spoofable) XFF entry.

geo.description: Country-level allow/deny rules (requires a GeoIP provider).

asn.description: ASN allow/deny rules (requires an ASN provider).

on_deny.description: Response to return when a request is denied in `enforce` mode.

NetworkMode.description: Operating mode for a [`NetworkPolicy`]. Defaults to `enforce`.

oneOf.description: Block requests that fail any rule, returning the `on_deny` response. Default.

Never block. Evaluate every rule and log what *would* have been blocked.
Use this to validate a policy against live traffic before enforcing it
(equivalent to a WAF "count"/"log-only" mode).

IpFilterSource.description:
Which IP field from the incoming request is used as the authoritative source
when evaluating `allow` and `deny` rules in an [`IpRule`].

Defaults to `client` when not specified.

oneOf.description: Leftmost X-Forwarded-For entry (best-guess real client). Default.
Raw TCP socket peer address (reliable when no proxy is involved).
X-Real-IP header value, falling back to `client` if not present.

allow.description:
Allowed IPs / CIDR ranges. If set, only matching requests pass (evaluated
against the policy `source` IP). Across levels these intersect.

deny.description:
Blocked IPs / CIDR ranges. Matching requests are denied. Across levels these
union (a config-level deny always applies). `deny_check` controls scope.

deny_check.description:
Which IPs are checked against `deny`: `source` (default) checks only the
resolved source IP; `all` checks every IP in the request chain.

DenyCheckMode.description:
Controls which IPs are checked against the `deny` list.

Defaults to `source` when not specified.

oneOf.description: Only check the configured `source` IP against the deny list. Default.

Check deny rules against every IP in the full chain: client, socket,
X-Real-IP, and all X-Forwarded-For hops. Useful for blocking known bad
actors even when they route through proxies.

GeoRule.description:
Country-level (GeoIP) allow/deny rule. Requires a configured GeoIP provider.
Country codes are ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. "US", "GB"), case-insensitive.

allow.description: Allowed country codes. If set, only requests resolving to one of these pass.

deny.description: Denied country codes. Requests resolving to one of these are blocked.

require_resolution.description:
When the country cannot be resolved: `true` blocks (fail-closed), `false`
(default) skips the rule (fail-open).

AsnRule.description: Autonomous System Number allow/deny rule. Requires a configured ASN provider.

allow.description: Allowed ASNs. If set, only requests resolving to one of these pass.

deny.description: Denied ASNs. Requests resolving to one of these are blocked.

require_resolution.description: When the ASN cannot be resolved: `true` blocks, `false` (default) skips.

RateLimitRule.description: Per-identifier request rate limit using a sliding window.

per.description: What to bucket requests by. Defaults to `ip`.

window.description: Window length as a humantime string ("60s", "1m", "1h"). Defaults to "60s".

burst.description:
Optional short-term burst allowance above `requests`. Reserved for the
token-bucket backend; ignored by the sliding-window default.

RateLimitScope.description: Identifier a [`RateLimitRule`] buckets requests by. Defaults to `ip`.

oneOf.description: Bucket by the policy's resolved `source` IP address. Default.

DenyResponse.description: Response returned when a request is denied in `enforce` mode.

status.description: HTTP status code. Defaults to 403. Set to 404 to hide a route's existence.

headers.description: Extra response headers to attach (e.g. `Retry-After` for rate limits).

McpTool.description:
Opt-in MCP tool exposure for an interface. See [`Interface::mcp`].

The MCP server auto-generates the tool's `inputSchema` from the interface's
`assert` tests (the same schema that powers the OpenAPI docs), so no separate
schema needs to be authored here.

enabled.description: Expose this interface as an MCP tool. Default `false`.

tool_name.description: Override the tool name shown to MCP clients. Defaults to the interface name.

description.description: Override the tool description. Defaults to the interface `summary`/`description`.

synchronous.description:
SQLite-only: `PRAGMA synchronous` (`OFF` | `NORMAL` | `FULL` | `EXTRA`). Defaults to `NORMAL`
(with WAL journaling) when unset — durable against application/process crashes and ~10x faster
than `FULL` on writes. Set `FULL`/`EXTRA` for maximum durability, or `OFF` for maximum speed
(no crash durability). Ignored by non-SQLite drivers.
